title: Bridge Essay: Psilocybin induces rapid and persistent growth of dendritic spines in frontal cortex in vivo.
concept: Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.
**Phase context:** Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

**Psilocybin induces rapid and persistent growth of dendritic spines in frontal cortex in vivo.** — Neuron (2021). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2021.06.008. PMID: 34228959. Psilocybin is a serotonergic psychedelic with untapped therapeutic potential. There are hints that the use of psychedelics can produce neural adaptations, although the extent and timescale of the impact in a mammalian brain are unknown. In this study, we used chronic two-photon microscopy to image longitudinally the apical dendritic spines of layer 5 pyramidal neurons in the mouse medial frontal cortex.
